text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet little village known as Cedar Grove, nobody talked about their true abilities. That was because everyone in Cedar Grove had a talent—some just didn't realize it yet. For instance, old Mr. Jenkins could make the rainbows stretch across the sky.

One summer day while weaving mats from dried grasses, Mrs. Jenkins whispered, "Mr. Jenkins? Look at the sky."

He paused and tilted his head towards a radiant arc. "There. A rainbow!" he declared.

The villagers thought it was just an illusion or maybe just a trick of the light. Until one night when no breeze stirred the moonlit leaves.

Mr. Jenkins muttered something about "special rainbows." Soon after, whispers spread among the youngsters.

"You've seen it?" they'd ask.

"A rainbow made from feelings. Just listen."

The youngsters waited. Nothing happened.

"Maybe next time," Mr. Jenkins said with a smile.

That night, he sat on his porch,
